perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder (other than Section 4(b)) shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (i) twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, (ii) such time as Receiving Party is no longer in possession of Blueknight Non-public Information and (iii) with respect to any Section of this Agreement (other than Section 4(b)), such earlier time as set forth in such Section.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder (other than Section 4(b)) shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (i) twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, (ii) such time as Receiving Party is no longer in possession of Blueknight Non-public Information and (iii) with respect to any Section of this Agreement (other than Section 4(b)), such earlier time as set forth in such Section. Notwithstanding the above, this agreement shall continue to remain in effect till the completion of the refinancing.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction in Section 5(c) regarding the termination of the agreement. The original clause lists specific conditions under which the agreement terminates. The added clause states that the agreement remains in effect until the completion of the refinancing. This creates an ambiguity because the refinancing may take longer than twenty days or the Receiving Party may no longer possess Blueknight Non-public Information before the refinancing is complete. This in-text contradiction leads to uncertainty about when the agreement actually terminates and is a contradiction to all the sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder (other than Section 4(b)) shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (i) twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, (ii) such time as Receiving Party is no longer in possession of Blueknight Non-public Information and (iii) with respect to any Section of this Agreement (other than Section 4(b)), such earlier time as set forth in such Section. Notwithstanding the above, this agreement shall continue to remain in effect till the completion of the refinancing. Section 4(b) of this Agreement and the obligations of the parties under Section 4(b) shall terminate upon the date that is ten (10) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause stating that the agreement remains in effect until the completion of the refinancing, despite the original clause listing specific conditions for termination (20 days, no possession of information, or as set forth in a specific section). This directly contradicts the original termination conditions because the refinancing may take longer than twenty days or the Receiving Party may no longer possess Blueknight Non-public Information before the refinancing is complete. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding when the agreement terminates, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall (a) keep and maintain any Material Blueknight Non-public Information in strict confidence and (b) not disclose to any other person the fact that Blueknight Non-public Information has been made available to Receiving Party, except, in each case, to the extent that Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including any filings required (and disclosures contained therein) under Section 13(d) of the Exchange Act) or by legal process (including by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, or similar process) to disclose any such information; provided, however, that Receiving Party may disclose such information to such of its representatives who need such information for the purpose of evaluating or assisting with Receiving Party’s investment in Blueknight, it being understood that such representatives shall be informed in advance by Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the provisions of this letter by such representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall (a) keep and maintain any Material Blueknight Non-public Information in strict confidence and (b) not disclose to any other person the fact that Blueknight Non-public Information has been made available to Receiving Party, except, in each case, to the extent that Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including any filings required (and disclosures contained therein) under Section 13(d) of the Exchange Act) or by legal process (including by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, or similar process) to disclose any such information; provided, however, that Receiving Party may disclose such information to such of its representatives who need such information for the purpose of evaluating or assisting with Receiving Party’s investment in Blueknight, it being understood that such representatives shall be informed in advance by Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the provisions of this letter by such representatives. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Receiving Party may disclose such Blueknight Non-Public Information to any potential buyers.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction in Section 3 regarding confidentiality. The original text strictly limits disclosure of Blueknight Non-public Information. The added sentence allows the Receiving Party to disclose such information to any potential buyers, directly contradicting the earlier confidentiality requirements and the original limited exceptions. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes about the scope of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall (a) keep and maintain any Material Blueknight Non-public Information in strict confidence and (b) not disclose to any other person the fact that Blueknight Non-public Information has been made available to Receiving Party, except, in each case, to the extent that Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including any filings required (and disclosures contained therein) under Section 13(d) of the Exchange Act) or by legal process (including by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, or similar process) to disclose any such information; provided, however, that Receiving Party may disclose such information to such of its representatives who need such information for the purpose of evaluating or assisting with Receiving Party’s investment in Blueknight, it being understood that such representatives shall be informed in advance by Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the provisions of this letter by such representatives. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Receiving Party may disclose such Blueknight Non-Public Information to any potential buyers. 4. Trading in Blueknight Securities; Refinancing Meeting .
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence allowing disclosure to 'any potential buyers,' which directly contradicts the initial confidentiality requirements in the Receiving Party clause, which aims to maintain strict confidence. This violates the definition of Inconsistencies in Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Each of Blueknight, the General Partner and Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each of the parties shall be entitled, without the requirement of posting a bond or other security, to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Each of Blueknight, the General Partner and Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each of the parties shall be entitled, without the requirement of posting a bond or other security, to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. However, for breaches of Section 3, money damages will be the sole remedy available.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction in Section 5(b) regarding remedies for breach of contract. The original clause states that specific performance and injunctive relief are available for any breach. The added sentence limits the remedy for breaches of Section 3 (confidentiality) to money damages only. This creates uncertainty about the remedies available for breaches of the confidentiality provisions. The In-text contradiction can be found within Section 5(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Each of Blueknight, the General Partner and Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each of the parties shall be entitled, without the requirement of posting a bond or other security, to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. However, for breaches of Section 3, money damages will be the sole remedy available. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the non-prevailing party as determined by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final judgment shall be liable and pay to the prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses such prevailing party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence to Section 5(b) that limits the remedy for breaches of Section 3 to money damages only, while the original text states that specific performance and injunctive relief are available for any breach of the agreement. This creates a clear contradiction within Section 5(b) itself, as the remedies available are now inconsistent. The contradicted text is the entire text of Section 5(b) in its modified form, as the added sentence directly conflicts with the initial statement regarding available remedies.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party hereby acknowledges that it is aware that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has Material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party hereby acknowledges that it is aware that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has Material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information. However, Receiving Party is authorized to trade in Blueknight securities during the restricted period, as long as such trades are pre-approved by Blueknight's General Counsel.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds a contradiction to Section 4(a). Initially, the section acknowledges insider trading restrictions. The added clause then seemingly authorizes the Receiving Party to trade in Blueknight securities, provided the trades are pre-approved. This directly contradicts the initial statement and introduces uncertainty regarding the application of insider trading laws and also is a contradiction in terms with Section 4(c) where restrictions are put in place, and also this is an in-text contradiction because the paragraph talks about the security laws in the beginning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party hereby acknowledges that it is aware that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has Material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information. However, Receiving Party is authorized to trade in Blueknight securities during the restricted period, as long as such trades are pre-approved by Blueknight's General Counsel. (b) The parties agree that on March 3, 2011, representatives of the parties intend to meet with each other as well as certain other limited partners of Blueknight to discuss the refinancing and recapitalization of Blueknight (the “Refinancing Meeting”).
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause authorizing the Receiving Party to trade in Blueknight securities with pre-approval, which directly contradicts the initial statement in both the changed text itself and the contradicted text (which are the same), which acknowledges awareness of US securities laws prohibiting insider trading. This inconsistency creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, as it is unclear whether insider trading laws apply or if pre-approved trades are exempt. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be transferred or assigned (by operation of law or otherwise) by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of Blueknight, and shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be transferred or assigned (by operation of law or otherwise) by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of Blueknight, and shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any of its Affiliates without Blueknight's consent.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction in Section 5(d) regarding assignment of the agreement. The first sentence prohibits assignment by the Receiving Party without Blueknight's consent. The added sentence allows assignment to Affiliates without consent. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes about the restrictions on assignment as there is an in-text contradiction, since the statement in the beginning, states that Blueknight consent is required, while the sentence being introduced nullifies this.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be subject to, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of New York (excluding its conflicts of laws rules that would require application of the laws of any jurisdiction other than the State of New York) and applicable federal laws and regulations. This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding among the parties concerning the subject matter hereof, and supersedes any prior agreements, written or oral, relating to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by Blueknight and Receiving Party expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement. This Agreement may not be transferred or assigned (by operation of law or otherwise) by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of Blueknight, and shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any of its Affiliates without Blueknight's consent. This Agreement may be executed by facsimile and in any number of counterparts, each of such counterparts shall for all purposes be deemed an original and all such counterparts shall together constitute but one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence that allows the Receiving Party to assign its rights and obligations to its Affiliates without Blueknight's consent, directly contradicting the original text which states that the agreement may not be transferred or assigned by the Receiving Party without Blueknight's prior written consent. This creates a direct conflict regarding the assignment of rights and obligations, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the explanation given. The contradicted text section is the same as the changed text but does not include the modification.
